name: Google Shape;84;p1
text:
Privileged access risk, explained and acted on.

Team xdcoderz
Adittya Sharma · Team Lead
Devyanshu Agrawal · Team Member
textPreview: Privileged access risk, explained and acted on. |  | Team xdcoderz | Adittya Sharma · Team Lead | Devyanshu Agrawal · Team Member

text: Detect unusual behavior before a valid login becomes an incident.
textPreview: Detect unusual behavior before a valid login becomes an incident.

text: Banks already control who can log in. The harder question begins after login: is this privileged user behaving normally? FinSpark Sentinel scores the session, explains the evidence, applies the least disruptive control, and records what happened.

name: Google Shape;91;p2
text: A valid privileged login can still become a high-impact incident after authentication.
textPreview: A valid privileged login can still become a high-impact incident after authentication.

text:
Confirms who entered
Issues a valid privileged session
Cannot judge every action in context
textPreview: Confirms who entered | Issues a valid privileged session | Cannot judge every action in context

text:
Unknown source · unfamiliar device
Credential access · audit disable
Privilege escalation · data staging
textPreview: Unknown source · unfamiliar device | Credential access · audit disable | Privilege escalation · data staging

text:
Payment disruption
Sensitive-data exposure
Slower investigation and response
textPreview: Payment disruption | Sensitive-data exposure | Slower investigation and response

text: Works beside the bank's existing PAM and SIEM; it does not replace them.
textPreview: Works beside the bank's existing PAM and SIEM; it does not replace them.

text: A valid credential does not guarantee safe behavior. FinSpark sits beside existing PAM and SIEM systems and closes the gap between authentication and impact.

name: Google Shape;99;p3
text: A bank pilot starts with mirrored events and controlled integrations—not a rip-and-replace program.
textPreview: A bank pilot starts with mirrored events and controlled integrations—not a rip-and-replace program.

text:
Logon and logoff
Commands and file activity
Source IP and device
Role, resource and privilege changes
textPreview: Logon and logoff | Commands and file activity | Source IP and device | Role, resource and privilege changes

text:
Read access to PAM / SIEM events
Sandbox or test enforcement endpoint
Identity and asset context
Approved data-retention boundary
textPreview: Read access to PAM / SIEM events | Sandbox or test enforcement endpoint | Identity and asset context | Approved data-retention boundary

text:
Named SOC and IAM reviewers
Threshold review cadence
Incident and rollback path
Observe-only entry phase
textPreview: Named SOC and IAM reviewers | Threshold review cadence | Incident and rollback path | Observe-only entry phase

text: A pilot needs only session telemetry, controlled access to PAM or IAM APIs, and agreed operating boundaries. We begin in observe-only mode and validate thresholds with analysts before enforcement.

name: Google Shape;106;p4
text: Every layer shown below is used by the working prototype or its reproducible Docker path.
textPreview: Every layer shown below is used by the working prototype or its reproducible Docker path.

text: Isolation Forest · weighted context · deterministic rules
textPreview: Isolation Forest · weighted context · deterministic rules

text: SQLAlchemy · Alembic · SQLite local / PostgreSQL path
textPreview: SQLAlchemy · Alembic · SQLite local / PostgreSQL path

text: Signed PAM adapter · liboqs · ML-KEM / ML-DSA in Docker
textPreview: Signed PAM adapter · liboqs · ML-KEM / ML-DSA in Docker

text: CERT-style insider-threat scenarios inform feature design; judge data is a transparent nine-session banking seed set.
textPreview: CERT-style insider-threat scenarios inform feature design; judge data is a transparent nine-session banking seed set.

text: This is the stack running in the prototype. The model is one component inside an API, policy, persistence, enforcement, and cryptographic control system.

name: Google Shape;113;p5
text: The repository explains how the system behaves, fails, deploys, and demonstrates its evidence.
textPreview: The repository explains how the system behaves, fails, deploys, and demonstrates its evidence.

text: Boundaries, event flow, adapters and scale path
textPreview: Boundaries, event flow, adapters and scale path

text: Assets, trust boundaries, attack paths and mitigations
textPreview: Assets, trust boundaries, attack paths and mitigations

text: Nine curated sessions and reproducible seed workflow
textPreview: Nine curated sessions and reproducible seed workflow

text: The repository is organized so judges can inspect not only the screens, but also architecture, threat assumptions, runbooks, demo data, migrations, and automated tests.

name: Google Shape;120;p6
text: The product earns trust by connecting risk evidence to a controlled response path.
textPreview: The product earns trust by connecting risk evidence to a controlled response path.

text: Five factor groups, not a mystery score
textPreview: Five factor groups, not a mystery score

text: Duplicate event IDs return the original decision
textPreview: Duplicate event IDs return the original decision

text: Adapter outcome and reference are persisted
textPreview: Adapter outcome and reference are persisted

text: OQS in Docker; compatibility mode labelled locally
textPreview: OQS in Docker; compatibility mode labelled locally

text: Our advantage is not another dashboard. It is the link from detection to accountable action, plus a conservative path from observation to policy-gated enforcement.

name: Google Shape;127;p7
text: One monorepo contains the API, analyst console, infrastructure, documents, and demo tooling.
textPreview: One monorepo contains the API, analyst console, infrastructure, documents, and demo tooling.

text: Assessment, policy, audit, vault and operations routes
textPreview: Assessment, policy, audit, vault and operations routes

text: The repository is a production-shaped monorepo. The demo routes, API documentation, health endpoint, and seeded-data workflow are all available from one checkout.

name: Google Shape;134;p8
text: FinSpark focuses first on privileged sessions where a single unsafe action can affect core banking services.
textPreview: FinSpark focuses first on privileged sessions where a single unsafe action can affect core banking services.

text:
Core ledger · customer data
Backup and export activity
Break-glass access
textPreview: Core ledger · customer data | Backup and export activity | Break-glass access

text:
Payment gateways · API fleets
Production recovery
High-risk command activity
textPreview: Payment gateways · API fleets | Production recovery | High-risk command activity

text:
Policy changes · key rotation
Service-account misuse
Privilege escalation
textPreview: Policy changes · key rotation | Service-account misuse | Privilege escalation

text: Decision agreement · false-positive review · response success · analyst handling time
textPreview: Decision agreement · false-positive review · response success · analyst handling time

text: The immediate banking use cases are high-impact privileged sessions: databases, payment platforms, and identity systems. Pilot value is measured by analyst agreement, response reliability, and false-positive review—not by vanity metrics.

name: Google Shape;141;p9
text: Our edge is not the risk score alone; it is the system behavior around that score.
textPreview: Our edge is not the risk score alone; it is the system behavior around that score.

text: Engine decision and analyst response separated
textPreview: Engine decision and analyst response separated

text: Unique event ID · idempotent replay · retry
textPreview: Unique event ID · idempotent replay · retry

text: OQS path in Docker; compatibility mode disclosed
textPreview: OQS path in Docker; compatibility mode disclosed

text: Most hackathon prototypes stop at a risk number. FinSpark implements the seams that make the number operational: idempotency, adapters, policy persistence, audit separation, and honest cryptographic status.

name: Google Shape;148;p10
text: The console keeps the analyst on one session record from alert to documented response.
textPreview: The console keeps the analyst on one session record from alert to documented response.

text: Compare baseline, timeline and factor weights
textPreview: Compare baseline, timeline and factor weights

text: Allow, step-up or block with an analyst note
textPreview: Allow, step-up or block with an analyst note

text: Confirm enforcement status and audit entry
textPreview: Confirm enforcement status and audit entry

text: The analyst stays in one evidence trail: triage, inspect baseline deviation and risk factors, choose a response, then confirm the recorded outcome.

name: Google Shape;155;p11
text: Keep the pilot operable today; separate high-volume components only when evidence justifies it.
textPreview: Keep the pilot operable today; separate high-volume components only when evidence justifies it.

text:
Single deployable API
Shared policy and persistence
Clear adapter interfaces
textPreview: Single deployable API | Shared policy and persistence | Clear adapter interfaces

text: Stateless API replicas · shared PostgreSQL · event queue · model workers · regional enforcement connectors
textPreview: Stateless API replicas · shared PostgreSQL · event queue · model workers · regional enforcement connectors

text: Route context by tenant and region
textPreview: Route context by tenant and region

text: Adapter per PAM, IAM or database control plane
textPreview: Adapter per PAM, IAM or database control plane

text: The current modular monolith is deliberate. Stateless APIs can share PostgreSQL, ingestion can move behind a queue, and model workers or enforcement adapters can scale independently when volume requires it.

name: Google Shape;162;p12
text: The same codebase supports a fast judge run, a reproducible Docker demo, and a controlled bank pilot.
textPreview: The same codebase supports a fast judge run, a reproducible Docker demo, and a controlled bank pilot.

text:
SQLite · compatibility crypto
Sandbox PAM adapter
One-command deterministic seed
Best for fast product walkthrough
textPreview: SQLite · compatibility crypto | Sandbox PAM adapter | One-command deterministic seed | Best for fast product walkthrough

text:
Compose-managed API and web
OQS runtime path
Reproducible dependencies
Health and readiness checks
textPreview: Compose-managed API and web | OQS runtime path | Reproducible dependencies | Health and readiness checks

text:
PostgreSQL · hardened ingress
OIDC + mTLS
HSM / KMS key boundary
Append-only audit destination
textPreview: PostgreSQL · hardened ingress | OIDC + mTLS | HSM / KMS key boundary | Append-only audit destination

text: Local mode supports fast judging. Docker activates the complete OQS path. A bank pilot adds OIDC, mTLS, KMS or HSM keys, hardened ingress, and append-only audit storage.

name: Google Shape;169;p13
text: The model sits inside a layered control system; cryptographic and integration boundaries are shown honestly.
textPreview: The model sits inside a layered control system; cryptographic and integration boundaries are shown honestly.

text:
Protected-route role checks
API-key auth available
Judge mode can disable auth
textPreview: Protected-route role checks | API-key auth available | Judge mode can disable auth

text:
Behavior baseline
Explainable factors
Deterministic threat rules
textPreview: Behavior baseline | Explainable factors | Deterministic threat rules

text:
HMAC-signed adapter
Retries + idempotency
Persisted outcome reference
textPreview: HMAC-signed adapter | Retries + idempotency | Persisted outcome reference

text:
Decision + analyst action
ML-DSA path in OQS runtime
No unsupported immutable claim
textPreview: Decision + analyst action | ML-DSA path in OQS runtime | No unsupported immutable claim

text: Runtime truth: local compatibility mode is not labelled quantum-safe. Docker activates the OQS provider path.
textPreview: Runtime truth: local compatibility mode is not labelled quantum-safe. Docker activates the OQS provider path.

alt: FinSpark Sentinel PQC vault status view

text: We disclose boundaries clearly. The local runtime is compatibility mode; Docker activates ML-KEM and ML-DSA. The backend includes API-key authentication and protected-route role checks; the current judge run has authentication disabled by configuration.

name: Google Shape;176;p14
text: A privileged session becomes one traceable decision chain from telemetry to response and audit.
textPreview: A privileged session becomes one traceable decision chain from telemetry to response and audit.

text: Fitted runtime with explicit readiness status
textPreview: Fitted runtime with explicit readiness status

text: Sessions, policy, outcomes and analyst trail
textPreview: Sessions, policy, outcomes and analyst trail

text: One session produces one evidence chain: telemetry, baseline comparison, explainable risk, policy decision, PAM response, and audit record.

name: Google Shape;183;p15
text: The seeded UI below is backed by the same API, policy, database, and audit records used in the walkthrough.
textPreview: The seeded UI below is backed by the same API, policy, database, and audit records used in the walkthrough.

text: Show response status and replay-safe evidence
textPreview: Show response status and replay-safe evidence

text: This is the working product. The seeded data shown here is the same data available through the live API: nine sessions, five flagged, and three block decisions.

name: Google Shape;188;p16
text:
Make every privileged session
explainable and accountable.
textPreview: Make every privileged session | explainable and accountable.

name: Google Shape;189;p16
text: Team xdcoderz · Adittya Sharma · Devyanshu Agrawal
textPreview: Team xdcoderz · Adittya Sharma · Devyanshu Agrawal

name: Google Shape;191;p16
text: Our ask: validate the control loop with mirrored PAM / SIEM events and security-operations feedback.
textPreview: Our ask: validate the control loop with mirrored PAM / SIEM events and security-operations feedback.

text: Detect early · apply the least disruptive control · keep evidence
textPreview: Detect early · apply the least disruptive control · keep evidence

text: FinSpark does not replace bank identity, PAM, or incident response systems. It closes the decision gap between them. Our next step is a controlled pilot using mirrored PAM and SIEM events with security-operations feedback.
